text: Testing plays a crucial role in software development. Unit tests check individual parts of code, like functions or methods, to ensure they work correctly in isolation. Integration tests, on the other hand, verify that different components work together properly. A popular approach is test-driven development, or TDD, where developers write tests before the actual code. This practice helps catch problems early and guides better design. Together, these testing strategies build confidence in the software. When developers see that tests pass, they can be more certain that changes or new features won’t break existing functionality. Ultimately, thorough testing reduces bugs and makes software more reliable.
